I'm finally wrapping up the analysis to join them. Missing this connection feels like a real loss right now.

My brain finally gave out after that pointless task, so I'm canceling the sync. Honestly, my team needs me way more than this dead weight.

desires: Reconnect with my cohort_3 group before they feel I've disappeared or become unreliable.
Avoid the exhaustion and resentment of being a 'dead weight' that forces me to cancel our syncs.

goals.description: Send a direct, honest text to cohort_3 explaining I'm pulling out of the sync tonight because my brain is fried and I don't want to be a 'dead weight' for them.
goals.rationale: I already messaged them at 19:30 saying I can't make it, but they might not get the full picture or feel abandoned. My worth isn't about showing up when I can't do anything useful; it's about being upfront with my team so they know where I stand. If I stay silent, they'll assume I dropped it coldly, which hurts our closeness more than my current exhaustion does. I need to fix this boundary now before resentment builds.

goals.description: Skip the assigned reading entirely and head straight to dinner with close_friend_group.
goals.rationale: The deadline for the analysis section passed an hour ago, so grinding on the reading adds zero value right now. My friends are waiting, and spending time with them feels way more valuable than finishing a soft-reading task at 8 PM. I've wasted enough time on this assignment; I need to recharge socially before I crash completely.
